title: Tapeinidium luzonicum
intro: Tapeinidium luzonicum, also known as Bagalai or Talinis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tapeinidium luzonicum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or used in the form of a decoction or infusion to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Tapeinidium luzonicum is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. This plant belongs to the genus Tapeinidium, which comprises various species of terrestrial and epiphytic ferns. Tapeinidium luzonicum is native to Southeast Asia and is often found in tropical regions. The classification of Tapeinidium luzonicum places it within the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies. Further classification includes the order Polypodiales and the subclass Polypodiidae.
